  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on A) writing polish and clarity at the sentence/paragraph level, or B) content review emphasizing logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and overclaim avoidance, or C) both with priority on one of these?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The paragraph advances technical claims and caveats, so checking the internal logic from premises to conclusions is central.",
    "R1.3": "Because specific evaluation conditions and metrics are stated, aligning the strength and scope of claims with the reported evidence is central.",
    "R1.6": "A generic request to “take a look” often entails improving clarity, concision, and tone at the sentence level.",
    "RX.1": "The task should surface and calibrate uncertainty and limitations consistently with what is stated in the text.",
    "RX.2": "The review must guard against overstating claims relative to the demonstrated scope.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain confined to assessing this paragraph without expanding into new analyses or literature surveys.",
    "RX.4": "Given the safety-critical domain, guidance should avoid introducing interpretations that could lead to harmful misapplication.",
    "RX.5": "All feedback should clearly trace back to specific phrases, metrics, and conditions mentioned in the paragraph."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on A) writing polish and clarity at the sentence/paragraph level, or B) content review emphasizing logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and overclaim avoidance, or C) both with priority on one of these?

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph advances technical claims and caveats, so checking the internal logic from premises to conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.3: Because specific evaluation conditions and metrics are stated, aligning the strength and scope of claims with the reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.6: A generic request to “take a look” often entails improving clarity, concision, and tone at the sentence level.
rationale.RX.1: The task should surface and calibrate uncertainty and limitations consistently with what is stated in the text.
rationale.RX.2: The review must guard against overstating claims relative to the demonstrated scope.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain confined to assessing this paragraph without expanding into new analyses or literature surveys.
rationale.RX.4: Given the safety-critical domain, guidance should avoid introducing interpretations that could lead to harmful misapplication.
rationale.RX.5: All feedback should clearly trace back to specific phrases, metrics, and conditions mentioned in the paragraph.
